metadata.motto: Nabha Sparsham Deeptam (Touch the Sky with Glory)
metadata.current_chief: Check official IAF website for current CAS
metadata.rank_structure: Based on British Royal Air Force

general_facts.world_ranking: 4th largest air force in the world

commands.name: Western Air Command
commands.area_of_responsibility: Punjab, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, J&K, Ladakh, Chandigarh, Delhi

commands.name: Central Air Command
commands.area_of_responsibility: Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, Bihar, Jharkhand

commands.name: Southern Air Command
commands.area_of_responsibility: Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana

fighter_aircraft.name: Sukhoi Su-30MKI
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The Su-30MKI is the most potent fighter in the IAF and can perform the famous 'Pugachev's Cobra' maneuver. It's customized specifically for India with Israeli, French, and Indian avionics.

fighter_aircraft.name: Dassault Rafale
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The Rafale deal was India's largest-ever defense purchase at $8.7 billion. These jets participated in the Republic Day Parade 2021, performing the 'Vertical Charlie' formation.

fighter_aircraft.name: HAL Tejas (LCA)
special_features: Smallest and Lightest Multi-role Supersonic Fighter
fighter_aircraft.funfact: Tejas is the second supersonic fighter developed by India and took 33 years from conception to induction! It's named 'Tejas' meaning 'Radiance' in Sanskrit.

fighter_aircraft.name: Mikoyan MiG-29
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The MiG-29 was India's first 4th generation fighter. During Kargil War, MiG-29s provided crucial air cover with their superior radar capabilities.

fighter_aircraft.name: Mikoyan MiG-21 Bison
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The MiG-21 is called the 'Flying Coffin' due to its high accident rate, yet Wing Commander Abhinandan shot down a Pakistani F-16 with a MiG-21 Bison in 2019!

fighter_aircraft.name: Dassault Mirage 2000
fighter_aircraft.funfact: Mirage 2000s conducted the famous 'Operation Safed Sagar' strikes during Kargil War and the 2019 Balakot airstrikes against terrorist camps in Pakistan.

fighter_aircraft.name: SEPECAT Jaguar
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The Jaguar is nicknamed 'Shamsher' (Sword) in IAF service. It's the primary nuclear delivery platform and was used extensively in Kargil for precision strikes.

transport_aircraft.name: Boeing C-17 Globemaster III
transport_aircraft.funfact: India is the largest operator of C-17s outside the US. These giants evacuated thousands during the 2015 Yemen crisis (Operation Raahat) and 2021 Afghanistan evacuation.

transport_aircraft.name: Lockheed Martin C-130J Super Hercules
transport_aircraft.funfact: A C-130J created history by landing at Daulat Beg Oldie (DBO) - the world's highest airstrip at 16,614 feet near the China border!

transport_aircraft.name: Ilyushin Il-76
transport_aircraft.funfact: Il-76s are the workhorses of IAF transport fleet. They've been used in every major operation from disaster relief to war logistics.

transport_aircraft.name: Ilyushin Il-78 MKI
transport_aircraft.funfact: The Il-78 MKI enabled IAF's Sukhoi Su-30MKIs to fly non-stop to Australia for Exercise Pitch Black - over 10,000 km!

transport_aircraft.name: Antonov An-32
transport_aircraft.funfact: An-32 holds the record for the highest landing by a transport aircraft - at Daulat Beg Oldie! It's nicknamed 'Sutlej' in IAF service.

transport_aircraft.name: Dornier Do 228
transport_aircraft.funfact: The Do 228 is license-built by HAL in India. It was the first aircraft to land on the newly-built aircraft carrier INS Vikrant's deck during trials!

transport_aircraft.name: DRDO Airborne Early Warning and Control System (DRDO AEW&CS)
transport_aircraft.funfact: This is India's indigenous AEW&C system nicknamed 'Netra' (Eye). It broke the monopoly of US, Israel, and Russia in this critical technology.

transport_aircraft.name: Beriev A-50 Phalcon
transport_aircraft.funfact: The A-50 Phalcon deal was initially blocked by the US which pressured Israel. India eventually got them and they've been critical for border surveillance.

transport_aircraft.name: Boeing 737 BBJ (VVIP)
transport_aircraft.funfact: Called 'Air India One' when the President or PM is on board. These aircraft have the callsign 'Rajdoot' (Ambassador) and replaced the aging Boeing 747s.

transport_aircraft.name: Embraer ERJ 145 / Embraer 135
transport_aircraft.funfact: These aircraft serve as executive transport for senior military and government officials, providing point-to-point travel capability.

transport_aircraft.name: Airbus A319
transport_aircraft.funfact: These Airbus jets serve as backup VVIP aircraft and are used for large delegation transport during international visits.

helicopters.name: Boeing AH-64E Apache
helicopters.funfact: The Apache is considered the world's most lethal attack helicopter. India's Apaches are the most advanced 'AH-64E Guardian' variant with the latest Longbow radar.

helicopters.name: Boeing CH-47F Chinook
helicopters.funfact: Chinooks can lift the M777 ultra-light howitzers to remote mountain positions in minutes - a game-changer for India's mountain warfare capability.

helicopters.name: Mil Mi-17 / Mi-17V-5
helicopters.funfact: The Mi-17 is the backbone of IAF helicopter fleet. It's been used in every operation from Kargil to disaster relief. One variant is specifically converted for PM's travel.

helicopters.name: Mil Mi-26
special_features: Largest production helicopter in the world
helicopters.funfact: The Mi-26 is so large it can carry another helicopter inside! It's been used to airlift stranded vehicles and heavy machinery during floods.

helicopters.name: Mil Mi-35
helicopters.funfact: The Mi-35 is unique - it's an attack helicopter that can also transport troops! Called 'flying tank' for its heavy armor and firepower.

helicopters.name: HAL Dhruv (ALH)
helicopters.funfact: Dhruv means 'Pole Star' in Sanskrit. It's the first indigenous helicopter by HAL and forms the basis for the armed Rudra variant.

helicopters.name: HAL Rudra (ALH WSI)
helicopters.funfact: Rudra means 'Fierce Form of Shiva'. It's India's first indigenous attack helicopter and bridges the gap until LCH Prachand is fully inducted.

helicopters.name: HAL Light Combat Helicopter (LCH) Prachand
special_features: World's only attack helicopter designed for high altitude
helicopters.funfact: Prachand means 'Fierce'. It's the world's only attack helicopter specifically designed for high-altitude mountain warfare at 5,000+ meters, perfect for Siachen!

helicopters.name: HAL Chetak
helicopters.funfact: The Chetak has served IAF for over 55 years! It's license-built version of the French Alouette III and trained generations of helicopter pilots.

helicopters.name: HAL Cheetah
helicopters.funfact: The Cheetah holds the world record for highest landing by a helicopter - at 7,500 meters on the slopes of Everest! It's the workhorse of Siachen operations.

helicopters.name: HAL Light Utility Helicopter (LUH)
helicopters.funfact: LUH is designed to replace aging Chetak and Cheetah helicopters. It can operate from Siachen Glacier to hot deserts - a true all-terrain helicopter.

trainer_aircraft.name: BAE Systems Hawk Mk.132
armament: Can carry bombs and missiles for weapons training
trainer_aircraft.funfact: The Hawk is used by the famous Surya Kiran Aerobatic Team and the aircraft involved in Balakot strikes were piloted by Hawk-trained pilots.

trainer_aircraft.name: HAL HJT-36 Sitara
trainer_aircraft.funfact: Sitara means 'Star' in Hindi. It's designed to bridge the gap between basic and advanced training, filling a critical void in IAF's training pipeline.

trainer_aircraft.name: HAL HPT-32 Deepak
trainer_aircraft.funfact: The HPT-32 was grounded in 2009 after multiple fatal crashes attributed to engine problems. It was the first indigenous trainer aircraft.

trainer_aircraft.name: Pilatus PC-7 Mk.II
trainer_aircraft.funfact: The Pilatus was purchased after the HPT-32 grounding created a training crisis. Ironically, its purchase was controversial due to alleged corruption.

trainer_aircraft.name: HAL HTT-40
trainer_aircraft.funfact: HTT-40 was developed to replace foreign basic trainers and reduce import dependency. It's fully aerobatic and can train pilots for all stages.

trainer_aircraft.name: Kiran (HAL HJT-16)
trainer_aircraft.funfact: The Kiran was India's first indigenous jet trainer and served for over 50 years! It was used by the Surya Kiran team before Hawks replaced them.

uav_drones.name: IAI Heron
uav_drones.funfact: Heron UAVs have been used extensively along the India-Pakistan and India-China borders. They provided crucial intelligence during the 2020 Ladakh standoff.

uav_drones.name: IAI Harop
uav_drones.funfact: Harop is a 'suicide drone' that destroys enemy radar by diving into them. It was used by Azerbaijan against Armenian air defenses with devastating effect.

uav_drones.name: IAI Searcher II
uav_drones.funfact: Searcher II has been the workhorse UAV for IAF's tactical reconnaissance. It played a key role in post-surgical strike damage assessment in 2016.

uav_drones.name: DRDO Rustom-2 (TAPAS-BH-201)
uav_drones.funfact: TAPAS (Tactical Airborne Platform for Aerial Surveillance) is India's answer to imported drones. It aims to match capabilities of Israeli Heron.

uav_drones.name: DRDO Lakshya
uav_drones.funfact: Lakshya means 'Target' in Sanskrit. It's designed to be shot down during practice! But it's also recoverable and reusable up to 5 times.

uav_drones.name: DRDO Abhyas
uav_drones.funfact: Abhyas means 'Practice' in Sanskrit. It's designed to be an affordable target drone for testing India's air defense systems like Akash.

uav_drones.name: General Atomics MQ-9B Predator (On order)
uav_drones.type: High Altitude Long Endurance (HALE) Armed UAV
uav_drones.funfact: The MQ-9B will be India's first armed drone, capable of eliminating targets across borders. This $3 billion deal includes both Sea Guardian and Sky Guardian variants.

special_mission_aircraft.name: Dassault Falcon 2000
special_mission_aircraft.funfact: These aircraft are part of Project 'Saraswati' and can intercept and analyze enemy communications and radar signals from standoff distances.

special_mission_aircraft.name: Gulfstream III / IV
special_mission_aircraft.funfact: These modified business jets carry classified electronic warfare equipment and have been used for sensitive intelligence missions along India's borders.

special_mission_aircraft.name: Boeing P-8I Poseidon (Indian Navy)
special_mission_aircraft.funfact: Though operated by the Navy, P-8Is coordinate with IAF for maritime domain awareness. They detected a Pakistani submarine during heightened tensions in 2019.

fighter_aircraft.name: Supermarine Spitfire
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The legendary Spitfire was among the first aircraft of the newly independent Indian Air Force. It saw action in the 1947-48 Kashmir War.

fighter_aircraft.name: Hawker Tempest
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The Tempest was the most powerful piston-engine fighter in IAF service. It provided crucial ground support during the 1947 Kashmir operations.

fighter_aircraft.name: Hawker Hurricane
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The Hurricane was the first modern fighter in IAF service. It fought alongside Spitfires in WWII as part of the Royal Indian Air Force against Japan.

fighter_aircraft.name: de Havilland Vampire
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The Vampire was IAF's first jet aircraft! It had a unique twin-boom design and saw extensive action in the 1947-48 Kashmir War.

fighter_aircraft.name: de Havilland Mosquito
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The 'Wooden Wonder' was made mostly of wood! It was one of the fastest aircraft in IAF service during its time and used for photo reconnaissance.

fighter_aircraft.name: Dassault Ouragan (Toofani)
fighter_aircraft.funfact: Called 'Toofani' (Stormy) in IAF service, this was India's first French aircraft purchase. It saw action in the 1965 war against Pakistan.

fighter_aircraft.name: Dassault Mystère IV
fighter_aircraft.funfact: Mystère IVs were the stars of the 1965 and 1971 wars. Squadron Leader Trevor Keelor shot down a Pakistani F-86 Sabre in a Mystère, becoming a national hero.

fighter_aircraft.name: Hawker Hunter
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The Hunter was the most elegant fighter in IAF service. In 1971, four Hunters destroyed over 50 Pakistani tanks in a single mission during the Battle of Longewala!

fighter_aircraft.name: Folland Gnat
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The tiny Gnat was called 'Sabre Slayer' for shooting down multiple Pakistani F-86 Sabres in 1965. It was so nimble that larger jets couldn't keep up!

fighter_aircraft.name: HAL Ajeet
fighter_aircraft.funfact: Ajeet means 'Unconquerable' in Sanskrit. It was an indigenously improved version of the Gnat and was India's first modified/developed fighter jet.

fighter_aircraft.name: English Electric Canberra
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The Canberra served IAF for 50 years! A Canberra piloted by Sqn Ldr Manmohan Sehgal was the first IAF aircraft to land in Srinagar during the 1947 war.

fighter_aircraft.name: Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-21 (Original variants)
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The MiG-21 is the most produced supersonic fighter in history! Flying Officer Nirmal Jit Singh Sekhon won India's only wartime Param Vir Chakra fighting Sabres in a MiG-21.

fighter_aircraft.name: Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-23
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The MiG-23 was India's first variable-sweep wing ('swing wing') aircraft. Its wings could change angle from 16° to 72° during flight for different speeds!

fighter_aircraft.name: Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-25
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The MiG-25 'Foxbat' was the fastest aircraft in IAF! It flew over Pakistan at 65,000+ feet altitude where no Pakistani interceptor could reach it.

fighter_aircraft.name: Mikoyan-Gurevich MiG-27
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The MiG-27 was called 'Bahadur' (Brave) in IAF. Its fearsome GSh-6-30 cannon fired so fast (6,000 rounds/minute) that its recoil could slow the aircraft!

fighter_aircraft.name: Sukhoi Su-7
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The Su-7 was the backbone of IAF's ground attack force in 1971 war. It destroyed countless Pakistani tanks and positions despite high losses.

fighter_aircraft.name: HAL Marut
fighter_aircraft.funfact: The Marut (Spirit of the Storm) was India's first indigenously designed and built jet fighter! Designed by German Kurt Tank who designed the WWII Focke-Wulf Fw 190.

fighter_aircraft.name: North American F-86 Sabre
fighter_aircraft.funfact: India evaluated the F-86 but didn't procure it. Ironically, it became IAF's primary adversary - Pakistan operated them and lost many to IAF's Gnats and Hunters!

transport_aircraft.name: Douglas C-47 Dakota
transport_aircraft.funfact: The Dakota was the first aircraft to land in Srinagar in 1947, carrying Indian troops to defend against Pakistani raiders. It served IAF for 41 years!

transport_aircraft.name: Douglas C-54 Skymaster
transport_aircraft.funfact: The C-54 was used for VVIP transport including carrying India's first President. It was the largest aircraft in early IAF service.

transport_aircraft.name: Fairchild C-119 Packet
transport_aircraft.funfact: The C-119 'Flying Boxcar' could open its entire tail for loading. It dropped supplies to trapped troops during the 1962 Sino-Indian War in freezing Himalayan conditions.

transport_aircraft.name: Antonov An-12
transport_aircraft.funfact: The An-12 was the workhorse before the Il-76. It was used for the famous Tangail airdrop in 1971, the largest IAF paradrop operation in history.

transport_aircraft.name: de Havilland Canada DHC-4 Caribou
transport_aircraft.funfact: The Caribou could land on incredibly short runways in the mountains. It supplied remote Himalayan posts that no other aircraft could reach.

transport_aircraft.name: de Havilland Canada DHC-3 Otter
transport_aircraft.funfact: The Otter was perfect for remote operations. It could take off and land on grassfields, beaches, and unprepared strips in the Northeast.

transport_aircraft.name: Hawker Siddeley HS 748 (Avro)
transport_aircraft.funfact: The HS 748 was license-built by HAL and called 'Avro' in IAF. It served for over 50 years and was the primary aircraft for transporting senior officers.

transport_aircraft.name: Boeing 737
transport_aircraft.funfact: These were modified Boeing 737s for PM and President's travel. One aircraft was reportedly in poor condition when transferred from Indian Airlines!

transport_aircraft.name: Boeing 707
transport_aircraft.funfact: The Boeing 707 was the prestigious VVIP aircraft that carried PMs across the world. It was eventually replaced by Boeing 747s.

transport_aircraft.name: Boeing 747-400
transport_aircraft.funfact: Called 'Air India One', these 747s carried the PM and President globally. They were finally replaced by Boeing 777 and 737 BBJs.

helicopters.name: Sikorsky S-55
helicopters.funfact: The S-55 was IAF's first helicopter! It had a unique nose-mounted radial engine and was used for the first helicopter operations in the Himalayas.

helicopters.name: Sikorsky H-19
helicopters.funfact: The H-19 was the workhorse of early IAF helicopter operations. It rescued countless soldiers and civilians in the mountainous Northeast.

helicopters.name: Mil Mi-4
helicopters.funfact: The Mi-4 was India's first armed helicopter. It saw action in the 1971 war, attacking Pakistani positions with rockets and machine guns.

helicopters.name: Mil Mi-8
helicopters.funfact: The Mi-8 was the predecessor to the Mi-17 and established the template for Soviet/Russian medium helicopters. Many were later upgraded to Mi-17 standard.

helicopters.name: Westland Sea King (HAL)
helicopters.funfact: The Sea King was the VVIP helicopter for the President and PM for decades. A white Sea King with the Ashoka emblem was an iconic sight at Rashtrapati Bhavan.

helicopters.name: Bell 47
helicopters.funfact: The Bell 47 with its distinctive bubble canopy trained early IAF helicopter pilots. It's the iconic helicopter seen in old movies and TV shows worldwide.

trainer_aircraft.name: de Havilland Tiger Moth
trainer_aircraft.funfact: The Tiger Moth was the first aircraft type in the Royal Indian Air Force! Founded on 8 October 1932, the IAF started with just 4 pilots and 6 Tiger Moths.

trainer_aircraft.name: North American T-6 Harvard/Texan
trainer_aircraft.funfact: The Harvard trained more WWII pilots than any other aircraft! Generations of IAF pilots learned to fly in this legendary trainer.

trainer_aircraft.name: de Havilland Chipmunk
trainer_aircraft.funfact: The Chipmunk was used by Air Force Academy for decades. It's still flown by flying clubs and is beloved for its gentle handling.

trainer_aircraft.name: Percival Prentice
trainer_aircraft.funfact: The Prentice was a side-by-side seater that allowed instructor and student to sit together. It was used for initial pilot selection.

trainer_aircraft.name: HAL HT-2
trainer_aircraft.funfact: The HT-2 was the first aircraft designed and built in India! It trained over 2,000 IAF pilots during its service life.

trainer_aircraft.name: HAL Pushpak
trainer_aircraft.funfact: Pushpak means 'Mythical Flying Vehicle' from the Ramayana. Though mostly civilian, some were used for IAF flying clubs.

trainer_aircraft.name: Iskra TS-11
trainer_aircraft.funfact: The Polish Iskra (Spark) was a stop-gap trainer before Kiran became fully operational. It introduced many pilots to jet flying.

bomber_aircraft.name: Consolidated B-24 Liberator
bomber_aircraft.funfact: The B-24 was the largest aircraft in early IAF service. It was used to bomb Japanese positions in Burma during WWII as part of Royal Indian Air Force.

bomber_aircraft.name: Tupolev Tu-142
bomber_aircraft.funfact: Though operated by the Navy, the Tu-142 deserves mention. It was the largest aircraft in Indian military service and could patrol for 12+ hours non-stop!

reconnaissance_aircraft.name: de Havilland Mosquito PR.34
reconnaissance_aircraft.funfact: The Mosquito PR.34 was the unarmed reconnaissance version of the famous 'Wooden Wonder'. It flew high-altitude photo missions over uncharted territory.

reconnaissance_aircraft.name: Lockheed P-2 Neptune
reconnaissance_aircraft.funfact: The Neptune was primarily operated by the Indian Navy but provided maritime reconnaissance support. It was the first dedicated maritime patrol aircraft.

air_to_air_missiles.name: MBDA Meteor
air_to_air_missiles.funfact: The Meteor is considered the world's most advanced BVR missile with a 'no-escape zone' three times larger than competitors.

air_to_air_missiles.name: MBDA MICA
air_to_air_missiles.funfact: MICA comes in radar and infrared variants, making it extremely versatile for both close combat and long-range engagement.

air_to_air_missiles.name: Vympel R-77 (RVV-AE)
air_to_air_missiles.funfact: The R-77 with its distinctive lattice fins is the primary BVR weapon for IAF's Russian-origin fighters.

air_to_air_missiles.name: Vympel R-73
air_to_air_missiles.funfact: The R-73 can be targeted using the pilot's helmet - wherever the pilot looks, the missile goes! Wing Cdr Abhinandan used this to shoot down a Pakistani F-16.

air_to_air_missiles.name: MBDA ASRAAM
air_to_air_missiles.funfact: ASRAAM is one of the most maneuverable short-range missiles, capable of engaging targets behind the launching aircraft.

air_to_air_missiles.name: Rafael Python-5
air_to_air_missiles.funfact: The Python-5 is a 5th generation missile with full-sphere engagement capability - it can hit targets in any direction around the aircraft.

air_to_air_missiles.name: Rafael Derby
air_to_air_missiles.funfact: The Derby gives Tejas credible BVR capability and is being replaced by the indigenous Astra missile.

air_to_air_missiles.name: DRDO Astra
air_to_air_missiles.funfact: Astra is India's first indigenous BVR missile. Astra Mk2 will have range exceeding 150 km, putting it in the same class as the Meteor.

air_to_ground_missiles.name: BrahMos-A
air_to_ground_missiles.funfact: BrahMos is the world's fastest cruise missile! The air-launched version makes the Su-30MKI one of the most lethal strike aircraft globally.

air_to_ground_missiles.name: MBDA SCALP / Storm Shadow
air_to_ground_missiles.funfact: SCALP gives Rafale deep strike capability to hit fortified bunkers from safe distance. It uses terrain-following flight to avoid radar detection.

air_to_ground_missiles.name: DRDO NGARM (Rudram-1)
air_to_ground_missiles.funfact: Rudram is India's first indigenous anti-radiation missile that can home in on enemy radars and destroy them.

air_to_ground_missiles.name: Kh-31
air_to_ground_missiles.funfact: The Kh-31 is one of the few supersonic anti-radar missiles in the world, flying at Mach 3.5 to destroy enemy air defenses.

air_to_ground_missiles.name: Kh-29
air_to_ground_missiles.funfact: The Kh-29 is a heavy tactical missile that can destroy hardened targets like bunkers and bridges.

bombs_and_guided_munitions.name: SPICE 2000
bombs_and_guided_munitions.funfact: SPICE 2000 was used in the 2019 Balakot airstrikes. It can glide 60 km to target and hit within 3 meters accuracy.

bombs_and_guided_munitions.name: HAMMER (AASM)
bombs_and_guided_munitions.funfact: HAMMER has both GPS and infrared guidance, making it effective against moving targets. It can be released from any altitude.

bombs_and_guided_munitions.name: Paveway II/III LGB
bombs_and_guided_munitions.funfact: Paveway series were the first 'smart bombs' in IAF service. They were used extensively in Kargil War with devastating effect on Pakistani positions.

bombs_and_guided_munitions.name: KAB-1500
bombs_and_guided_munitions.funfact: The KAB-1500 is a massive bunker-buster that can penetrate over 2 meters of concrete. It's designed to destroy hardened underground targets.

bombs_and_guided_munitions.name: DRDO Sudarshan LGB
bombs_and_guided_munitions.funfact: Sudarshan converts 'dumb' bombs into precision weapons. Named after Lord Vishnu's divine discus, it's much cheaper than imported alternatives.

aerobatic_teams.name: Surya Kiran Aerobatic Team
aerobatic_teams.funfact: Surya Kiran is one of the few aerobatic teams in the world that flies 9 aircraft in formation. They've performed over 600 displays worldwide.

aerobatic_teams.name: Sarang Helicopter Display Team
aerobatic_teams.funfact: Sarang is the only helicopter aerobatic team in Asia! They perform the spectacular 'Sarang Split' where all 4 helicopters break away simultaneously.

special_forces.name: Garud Commando Force
special_forces.meaning: Garud - Mythical Eagle mount of Lord Vishnu
special_forces.selection: 3-week probation followed by 72 weeks of training
special_forces.funfact: Garud commandos undergo one of the toughest selection processes in India. Less than 10% candidates complete the grueling training. They were involved in the 2016 surgical strikes.

historic_operations.name: Operation Vijay
historic_operations.description: Liberation of Goa from Portuguese rule
historic_operations.funfact: IAF achieved complete air supremacy within 36 hours. Not a single IAF aircraft was lost.

historic_operations.name: 1965 Indo-Pakistani War
historic_operations.description: Aerial combat against Pakistan Air Force
historic_operations.funfact: The tiny Gnat earned the nickname 'Sabre Slayer' in this war.
